tools.name: PlugX RAT
tools.evidence_snippet: USB worm variants hide malicious files on infected USB devices in RECYCLER BIN directory; exfiltrates via C2; scans for more USBs; targets government agencies

tools.name: Hunters International Encryptor
tools.evidence_snippet: Shares 60% code with HIVE version 6 ransomware strain

tools.name: Raccoon Stealer
tools.evidence_snippet: Infostealer MaaS; steals browser autofill passwords/history/cookies, usernames/passwords, credit cards, crypto wallets; rented for ~$75/week

tools.name: IcedID
tools.evidence_snippet: Microsoft Defender Antivirus detects multiple variants

summary: Ontinue 1H 2024 Threat Intelligence Report (Jan-Jun 2024): LockBit most active ransomware; Hunters International (likely HIVE rebrand, 60% code overlap) targets healthcare/manufacturing/logistics; Manufacturing/Industrial attacks up to 41% (from 20% 2023); Top 10 trending CVEs (50% from 2023): CVE-2023-36025 (#1 SmartScreen bypass), CVE-2024-21887 (#2 Ivanti), CVE-2024-3400 (#3 PAN-OS), 3x Ivanti zero-days still exploited. Threat spotlights: LOLSites AiTM via powerappsportals.com (Microsoft domains); SharePoint phishing (compromised user creates fake file-share redirecting to harvester); Infostealers (Raccoon Stealer MaaS $75/wk); PlugX RAT USB worm (RECYCLER.BIN hiding, gov targets). Top ION detections: Honeytoken auth, ZAP-quarantined links, suspicious files, PlugX, AiTM sites. Chinese state-sponsored: info control focus, zero-day proliferation, contractor ops (i-Soon leak: 32 state customers, APT ops-for-hire). LockBit disrupted (Operation Cronos: 34 servers seized, decryptor released).

timeline.event: Data collection period begins; top 10 trending CVEs tracked from January 2024
Operation Cronos disrupts LockBit: servers seized, arrests in Poland/Ukraine, decryptor released
timeline.evidence_snippet: Recent operations by international law enforcement agencies have successfully disrupted the notorious LockBit ransomware group

timeline.event: Data collection period ends for 1H 2024 report
